title: haplopappus rigidus
intro: Haplopappus rigidus, also known as stiff hop or stiff hop plant, is a flowering plant species native to North America. It belongs to the sunflower family and is characterized by its tall stature, with rigid stems and leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to have various health benefits, including wound healing and reduction of fever. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by drying and processing the leaves and flowers of the Haplopappus rigidus plant.
